rationale: The home-load baseline is fully migrated into the shared benchmark fixture and remains benchmark-active.

The thesis-active auth benchmark retains the comparable sign-in task and excludes broader auth-state correctness checks that would compare session policy rather than locator robustness under UI change.
excludedCoverage: session persistence and invalid-token recovery checks

rationale: Comparable article-read and single-control state-change flows were migrated into the shared corpus; authoring and ownership workflows remain outside the thesis-active corpus because they compose many benchmarkable controls into one product-journey outcome.
The shared corpus retains the comparable add-comment and delete-own-comment tasks and excludes broader correctness/edge-case checks that do not add distinct locator-family evidence.

The shared corpus keeps the directly comparable global-feed and pagination tasks; broader route-walk and tag-surface checks are excluded because they are application-navigation correctness checks or depend on backend-specific feed/sidebar behavior.

The active corpus benchmarks one controlled profile-update task and excludes orthogonal field-persistence variants whose value is product-correctness coverage rather than additional locator-family evidence.

The thesis-active corpus retains the comparable follow-toggle task while excluding broader profile/feed visibility assertions that primarily measure backend content state rather than a controlled locator-family interaction.
This spec focuses on API error handling and resilience semantics rather than controlled locator-family robustness under non-breaking UI change.
Null-field normalization is backend/data-contract coverage rather than a locator robustness comparison task.
excludedCoverage: nullable field rendering and persistence semantics

rationale: URL-shape and deep-link routing checks validate navigation correctness, not locator-family robustness under injected UI change.
User-fetch failure handling probes infrastructure and resilience semantics, not the controlled UI-mutation question of the thesis benchmark.
Security and sanitization checks remain intentionally outside the thesis-active corpus because they address application security guarantees rather than locator robustness.
